en: A: I'm not sure about that, but I think it was during the 1990s ... I can check online.

zh: B: 好的，上面说他只有四十三岁......那真令人难过。
en: B: OK, so it says he was only forty-three ... that's sad.

zh: 我想我曾经读到过他在那之后放弃了他的职业生涯。
en: I think I read once that he gave up his career after that time.

zh: A: 我不这么认为。
en: A: I don't think so.

zh: 我相信他最终回到了他的职业生涯和慈善工作中。
en: I believe he returned to both his career and his charity work eventually.

zh: 事故非常严重，他进行了许多锻炼，但他确实重新工作了，并为慈善事业筹集了大量资金。
en: The accident was very severe and he engaged in many exercises but he did work again and raised lots of money for charity.

zh: 这是一部非常感人和美丽的电影。
en: This is an incredibly moving and beautiful film.

zh: 它展现了一位父亲最极致的爱。
en: It shows a father's love at its very best.

zh: 这是一部催人泪下的电影，我必须承认我哭得心碎。
en: It is a tear-jerker and I must admit to sobbing my heart out.

zh: 这是一个设定在1939年第二次世界大战开始时的故事。
en: It is a story set in 1939 at the start of World War II.

zh: 圭多，一位充满活力和快乐的意大利犹太侍者，与不是犹太人的多拉结婚。
en: Guido, an exuberant and cheerful Italian-Jewish waiter, gets married to Dora, who is not Jewish.

zh: 他们相爱并有一个名叫乔舒亚的儿子。
en: They are in love and have a son named Giosué.

zh: 当他们正在为乔舒亚的五岁生日做准备时，圭多和乔舒亚被捕并被带到一个集中营。
en: As they are preparing for Giosué's fifth birthday, Guido and Giosué are arrested and taken to a concentration camp.

zh: 多拉恳求和他们一起去，尽管她不是犹太人。
en: Dora begs to go with them even though she is not Jewish.

zh: 她被送到营地另一边的妇女区。
en: She is sent to the women's section on the other side of the camp.

zh: 为了向儿子隐藏可怕的真相，圭多假装他们在一个假日营地并且正在玩游戏。
en: To hide the awful truth from his son, Guido pretends they are at a holiday camp and are playing a game.

zh: 在游戏中，他们必须赢得积分。
en: In the game, they have to win points.

zh: 他告诉儿子，目标是获得1000分——奖品是一辆真正的坦克，他们可以乘坐它离开。
en: He tells his son that the objective is to get 1,000 points — and the prize is a real tank in which they can leave.

zh: 这里，他指的是属于盟军的坦克，他希望盟军最终能在赢得战争时来拯救他们离开集中营。
en: Here, he is referring to the tanks belonging to the allies that he hopes will eventually come to save them from the concentration camp when they win the war.

zh: 他假装乔舒亚可以通过不哭、不说他饿了、不找妈妈等等事情来获得积分。
en: He pretends Giosué can get points for things such as not crying, not saying he is hungry, not asking for his mother, and so on.

zh: 最终，圭多把乔舒亚藏起来，并一直假装这是一个游戏，他们在玩捉迷藏。
en: Eventually, Guido hides Giosue, all the while pretending it is a game and they are playing hide-and-seek.

zh: 我不会告诉你电影是如何结束的。
en: I won't tell you how the film ends.

zh: 你必须自己去看。
en: You will have to watch it for yourself.

zh: 许多评论家推荐了这部电影，并说这是一部必看之作。
en: Many critics have recommended this film, and said that it is a must-see.

zh: 我完全同意，因为它是我看过的最美丽的电影之一——一部我永远不会忘记的电影。
en: I completely agree, as it is one of the most beautiful films I have ever seen — one that I will never forget.

zh: 我们用过去将来时来谈论从过去某个时间点看将来会发生的事情。
en: We use the past future to talk about what will happen in the future from the point of some time in the past.

zh: Will 和 be going to 用来谈论一般将来时。
en: Will and be going to are used to talk about the present future.

zh: 在过去将来时中，will 变为 would，be going to 变为 was / were going to。
en: We turn will into would, be going to into was / were going to in the past future.

zh: 以下句子展示了过去将来时的构成：
en: These sentences illustrate how the past future is formed:

zh: 很少有人知道未来的虚拟化身会有如此广泛的形式和用途。
en: Very few people knew that future avatars would have such a wide variety of forms and uses.

zh: 他们发现他们将拥有创造新身份的力量。
en: They discovered that they were going to have the power to create new identities.

zh: would 用于过去将来时和表示过去重复的动作时意义不同。
en: The use of would in the past future is different to the use of would for repeated actions in the past.

zh: 他说他会在会议上提出这个问题。
en: He said he would raise the question at the meeting.

zh: 当我小的时候，我妈妈晚上会给我读故事。
en: When I was small my mum would read me stories at night.

zh: 我们将两个现有的词连接在一起形成一个复合词。
en: We link two existing words together to form a compound word.

zh: 通常，第一个词表示第二个词的主要特征。
en: Normally, the first word gives us a key feature of the second word.

zh: 第二个词体现复合词的词性。
en: The second word then describes the part of speech of the compound word.

zh: 复合词有不同词性，如形容词 (online, self-centred, real-life), 名词 (friendship, percent, website) 和动词 (babysit, overdo)。
en: The compound words appear in different word classes, such as adjectives (online, self-centred, real-life), nouns (friendship, percent, website) and verbs (babysit, overdo).

zh: 关系副词 where 和 when 引导的定语从句提供地点和时间方面的信息。
en: Relative adverbs where and when are used to give information about places and time.

zh: 这就是我丢失钱包的地方。
en: This is the place where I lost my purse.

zh: 马可·波罗游历到中国，那里当时有许多发明和发展。
en: Marco Polo travelled to China where there were inventions and developments at that time.

zh: 她仍然记得比尔第一次走进她办公室的那一天。
en: She still remembers the day when Bill first walked into her office.

zh: 我永远不会忘记我第一次见到这位伟大发明家的那一天。
en: I'll never forget the day when I first met this great inventor.

zh: 你知道他为什么对我这么生气吗？
en: Do you know the reason why he's so angry with me?

zh: 你能告诉我为什么你在可以在网上办理银行业务的情况下还要浪费时间去银行吗？
en: Could you tell me the reason why you waste time going to the bank when you can do your banking online?

zh: 他咳嗽得这么厉害的原因是雾霾。
en: The reason (that) he's coughing so badly is because of the smog.

zh: 这就是我买那本书的商店。
en: This is the shop from which I bought the book.

zh: 他就是和我讨论我项目的老师。
en: He is the teacher with whom I discussed my project.

zh: 我乘坐的这趟火车是开往上海的。
en: The train on which I'm travelling is for Shanghai.

zh: 这就是那位我从他身上学到了很多东西的教授。
en: This is the professor from whom I've learnt a lot.

zh: 她正在做一个我知之甚少的重要项目。
en: She's doing an important project about which I know little.

zh: 你见过我用它来研究天空的那台望远镜吗？
en: Have you seen the telescope with which I studied the sky?

zh: 同义词是意义几乎相同的一组词语。
en: Synonyms are words that have nearly the same meaning as other words in the same language.

zh: 它们词性相同。
en: They possess the same part of speech.

zh: 反义词是意义相反的一组词语。
en: Antonyms are words that carry opposite meanings.

zh: 和同义词一样，它们词性相同。
en: Similar to synonyms, they carry the same part of speech.

zh: 反义词可以通过给词根添加前缀/后缀构成。
en: Antonyms can be formed by adding prefixes / suffixes to the root words.

zh: 科技真的能使人们更好地相互交流。
en: Technology really enables people to communicate better with each other.

zh: 他们期待着有朝一日，自己的虚拟形象会像真人一样去环游更广阅忆更精彩的虚拟世界。
en: They look forward to a time when their avatar will act like a real person and travel around bigger, more exciting virtual worlds.

zh: 正是这些技能使我们能够发展终身的友谊。
en: It is these skills that enable us to develop lifelong friendships.

zh: 一些人不再和朋友好好地交谈了，他们只关心自己网上的人气——我得了多少个“赞”？
en: Instead of having proper conversations with their friends, some people are only concerned with their online popularity — How many "likes" did I get?

zh: 野生动物的灭绝不给你时间改正错误，在科学面前，我们没有机会去改过。
en: Wildlife extinction is not a process that gives you time to fix your mistakes. In the face of science, there are no second chances.

zh: 突然，天空乌云密布，不到一分钟，我们就陷入了一场可怕的风暴中。
en: All at once, the sky was covered with dark clouds and in less than a minute we were in a terrible storm.

zh: 最后，一条船上的人把我救了起来。
en: In the end, a boat picked me up.

zh: 首先，他的两个雪橇坏了，然后马匹开始因冰雪和严寒而遇到严重困难。
en: First, his two sledges broke down and then the horses began to have serious difficulties with the snow and the cold.

zh: 然后他准备返回。
en: Then he prepared for the return journey.

zh: 队员们很快就精疲力竭，食物也快吃完了。
en: The men were soon very tired and were running out of food.

zh: 后来，这些岩石证明在遥远的过去，南极洲大陆曾被植物覆盖。
en: Later, these rocks proved that at one time in the distant past, the continent of Antarctica was covered by plants.

zh: 曾经他与他的叔叔住在一起。
en: At one time he lived with his uncle.

zh: 斯科特上校和两名队员继续向前，到达距离食品储备处不足11英里的地方。
en: Scott and two of his team members carried on and got within eleven miles of one of their food bases.

zh: 她停下来往后看了看，又继续下楼。
en: She stopped and looked back, then carried on down the stairs.

zh: 尽快停车，但是不要停靠在建筑物附近或者树下。
en: Stop your car as soon as you can, but avoid stopping near a building or under a tree.

zh: 为什么不去做我们热衷的事情呢？
en: Why do we avoid doing what we love to do?

zh: 我觉得自己活着，就是为了使别人过得更美好。
en: The aim of living is making better lives for others.

zh: 她开始谈论起救命药物青蒿素，这是她在20世纪70年代在团队的帮助下发现的。
en: She began to talk about the life-saving drug, artemisinin, which she had discovered with the help of her team in the 1970s.

zh: 这位女士就是屠呦呦，是中国第一位获得诺贝尔奖的女科学家。
en: The woman was Tu Youyou, the first Chinese female scientist to be awarded a Nobel Prize for her work.

zh: 诺贝尔奖，根据已故瑞典化学家阿尔弗雷德·诺贝尔的遗嘱而设立。
en: Nobel Prize is established according to the will of the late Swedish chemist Alfred Nobel.

zh: 分设物理学、化学、生理学或医学、文学、和平五个奖项。
en: It is divided into five awards: physics, chemistry, physiology or medicine, literature, and peace.

zh: 诺贝尔奖1901年开始颁发，每年一次。
en: The Nobel Prize was first awarded in 1901 and is given annually.

zh: 1968年起增设经济学奖。
en: The Economics Prize was added in 1968.

zh: 莫罕达斯·卡拉姆昌德·甘地（1869—1948），印度民族运动的领导人，在印度被尊称为“圣雄甘地”。
en: Mahatma Gandhi (1869-1948) was a leader of the Indian national movement and is revered in India as 'Mahatma Gandhi'.

zh: 马丁·路德·金（1929—1968），美国黑人民权运动领袖。
en: Martin Luther King (1929-1968) was a leader of the African-American civil rights movement in the United States.

zh: 1955—1956年间，他领导了阿拉巴马州蒙哥马利市的黑人罢乘公共汽车活动，获得成功，从而成为民权运动的领导者。
en: From 1955 to 1956, he led the Montgomery bus boycott in Alabama, which was successful, making him a leader of the civil rights movement.

zh: 1963年8月，他组织集会，反对种族歧视，在林肯纪念堂前发表了题为“我有一个梦想”的著名演说。
en: In August 1963, he organized a rally against racial discrimination and delivered his famous 'I Have a Dream' speech in front of the Lincoln Memorial.

zh: 马丁·路德·金主张非暴力主义，多次被捕入狱。
en: Martin Luther King advocated nonviolence and was arrested and imprisoned multiple times.

zh: 1968年4月4日，他被种族主义分子杀害。
en: On April 4, 1968, he was assassinated by a racist.

zh: 华盛顿哥伦比亚特区，全称“华盛顿哥伦比亚特区”，简称为华盛顿，美国首都。
en: Washington, D.C., the full name is 'Washington, District of Columbia', abbreviated as Washington, is the capital of the United States.

zh: 位于美国马里兰州和弗吉尼亚州交界处。
en: It is located at the junction of Maryland and Virginia.

zh: 华盛顿于1790年被定为首都，为纪念美国第一任总统乔治·华盛顿。
en: Washington was designated the capital in 1790 in honor of the first President of the United States, George Washington.

zh: “超人”是美国DC漫画公司出版的一本漫画小说中的虚构角色，该角色此后出现在多部电影中。
en: Superman is a fictional character from a comic novel published by American DC Comics, and the character has since appeared in many films.

zh: 其最早一次亮相是在1951年的《超人与摩尔人》电影中。
en: His earliest appearance was in the 1951 film 'Superman and the Mole Men'.

zh: 克里斯托弗·里夫饰演了四部《超人》电影，分别是《超人》（1978年）、《超人2》（1980年）、《超人3》（1983年）、《超人4》（1987年）。
en: Christopher Reeve starred in four 'Superman' films: 'Superman' (1978), 'Superman II' (1980), 'Superman III' (1983), and 'Superman IV: The Quest for Peace' (1987).

zh: 克里斯托弗·里夫（1952-2004），美国演员、导演和制片人。
en: Christopher Reeve (1952-2004) was an American actor, director, and producer.

zh: 他初登银幕是在1976年，出演《重力事件》中的配角。
en: He first appeared on screen in 1976, playing a supporting role in the film 'Gray Lady Down'.

zh: 1978—1987年间，克里斯托弗·里夫主演了四部《超人》，也由此赢得了英国电影学院最佳男主角新人奖。
en: From 1978 to 1987, Christopher Reeve starred in four 'Superman' films, for which he won the BAFTA Award for Most Promising Newcomer.

zh: 2004年10月，克里斯托弗·里夫因心脏病逝世。
en: In October 2004, Christopher Reeve died of heart failure.

zh: 我喜欢探索新技术，因为它们非常方便。
en: I love exploring new technologies, because they are so convenient.

zh: 你知道，我用应用程序在网上买东西，而且我通常当天就能收到！
en: You know, I use apps to buy things online and I usually receive them the same day!

zh: 确实！我知道有一个甚至可以在两小时内帮你拿到你订购的东西！
en: Indeed! I know one that can even help you get what you order within two hours!

zh: 它是什么？我也想试试。
en: What is it? I'd like to try that, too.

zh: 除了网上购物，我也喜欢网上聊天。
en: Besides shopping online, I also like chatting online.

zh: 这让我感觉不那么孤单。
en: It makes me feel less lonely.

zh: 科技确实能让人们更好地相互交流。
en: Technology really enables people to communicate better with each other.

zh: 但是这些新技术有什么缺点吗？
en: But are there any disadvantages about these new technologies?

zh: 嗯，过多的网上聊天会花费很多时间。
en: Well, too much chatting online takes a lot of time.

zh: 我们真的需要时间来专注于我们正在做的事情。
en: We really need time to focus on what we are doing.

zh: 这部新手机是我父母送的生日礼物。
en: This new phone is a birthday present from my parents.

zh: 我已经下载了很多有趣又有用的应用程序。
en: I've downloaded lots of interesting and useful apps already.

zh: 我喜欢这个音乐应用程序——当我睡不着或感到紧张时，我可以听歌。
en: I like this music app — I can listen to songs when I can't sleep or feel nervous.

zh: 它真的帮助我放松。
en: It really helps me relax.

zh: 我喜欢在手机上读新闻。
en: I like reading news on my phone.

zh: 你有什么推荐吗？
en: Do you have any recommendation?

zh: 哦，是的！你可以下载这个应用程序！
en: Oh, yes! You can download this app!

zh: 你会一整天都收到新闻更新。
en: You'll get updated with the news all day long.

zh: 我已经下载了好几个不同游戏的应用程序。
en: I have downloaded quite a few apps for different games.

zh: 我在不同的游戏中使用不同的头像，这真的很酷，因为它们让我探索不同的个性。
en: I use different avatars in different games and this is really cool because they let me explore different personalities.

zh: 新游戏让我的生活变得有趣。
en: New games make my life interesting.

zh: 但你需要小心在上面花费太多时间——你可能会上瘾。
en: But you need to be careful about spending too much time on them — you could get addicted.

zh: 所以我下载了一个叫“闹钟”的应用程序。
en: So I downloaded an app called "Alarm Clock".

zh: 一旦我玩游戏超过一个小时，铃声就会响起，提醒我时间到了。
en: Once I play games for more than an hour, a bell rings to remind me that my time is up.

zh: 欢迎来到7een科技访谈。
en: Welcome to 7een Tech Talk.

zh: 如今，我们在移动设备上使用许多软件应用程序。
en: Nowadays, we use many software applications on our mobile devices.

zh: 我们简称它们为应用程序。
en: We call them apps for short.

zh: 今天，我的嘉宾是李兴，来自新科技公司的一名应用程序开发员。
en: Today, my guest is Li Xing, an app developer from New-tech Company.

zh: L: 你好！谢谢邀请我。
en: L: Hello! Thanks for having me.

zh: I: 那么，在你看来，为什么应用程序如此受欢迎？
en: I: So, in your opinion, why are apps so popular?

zh: L: 嗯，应用程序非常有用。
en: L: Well, apps are very useful.

zh: 它们帮助我们找到受欢迎的餐厅、叫出租车、找到去不同地方的路线等等。
en: They help us find popular restaurants, call for taxis, find directions to different places, and so on.

zh: 它们让我们的生活方便多了，不是吗？
en: They make our life much easier, don't they?

zh: 由于我们的许多听众都是学生，你能告诉我们一些关于教育类应用程序的事情吗？
en: As many of our listeners are students, could you tell us something about educational apps?

zh: 教育类应用程序正变得越来越强大。
en: Educational apps are becoming increasingly powerful.

zh: 例如，一些应用程序允许学生根据他们的个人兴趣和需求选择他们的在线老师和科目。
en: For example, some apps allow students to choose their online teachers and subjects according to their personal interests and needs.

zh: 它们还确保所有学生都能享受到相同的学习材料，即使他们没有坐在同一个教室里！
en: They also ensure that students can all enjoy the same learning materials, even when they're not sitting in the same classroom together!

zh: 但是开发一个应用程序一定很困难，对吧？
en: But developing an app must be difficult, right?

zh: 最困难的事情是发现人们的需求并解决现实世界的问题。
en: The most difficult thing is finding out what people need and solving real-world problems.

zh: I: 解决现实世界的问题，这听起来确实很有挑战性。
en: I: Solving real-world problems, that does sound challenging.

zh: 你们现在正在开发什么应用程序？
en: What apps are you working on right now?

zh: L: 我们正在努力在我们的应用程序中使用最新技术，比如VR、AR和MR。
en: L: We're working on using the latest technologies in our apps, like VR, AR and MR.

zh: 让我们在休息之后继续这个讨论。
en: Let's continue this discussion after the break .=

zh: D: 陈宇，你忙吗？你能帮我个忙吗？
en: D: Chen Yu, are you busy? Can you help me out with something?

zh: D: 还记得你告诉我的那个购物应用程序吗？
en: D: Remember that shopping app you told me about?

zh: 你需要帮忙下载它吗？
en: Do you need help downloading it?

zh: D: 不，我已经下载了，但我不知道如何设置账户。
en: D: No, I've downloaded it, but | can't figure out how to set up an account.

zh: 我可以帮你。
en: | can help you with that.

zh: 首先，按下“注册”按钮。
en: First, press the "Sign Up" button.

zh: 那么现在我开始输入我的信息，对吗？
en: So now | start putting in my information, right?

zh: C: 是的，输入你的姓名、性别、电话号码和电子邮件地址。
en: C: Yes, put in your name, gender, telephone number and email address.

zh: 那么，都设置好了吗？
en: Is it all set up, then?

zh: 最后一步是按下“完成”按钮。
en: The last step is to press the "Finish" button.

zh: 好了，你现在应该可以开始购物了！
en: OK, you should now be ready to start shopping!

zh: 实际上，请稍等一下。
en: Actually, just hold on a second.

zh: 现在我不明白这是什么意思......
en: Now | don't understand what this means...

zh: 这里是ABC购物客服中心。
en: This is ABC Shopping Customer Service.

zh: 我可以怎样帮助您？
en: How may | help you?

zh: 我两周前使用你们的应用程序下了一个订单，但我仍然没有
en: | placed an order using your app two weeks ago, but | still haven't

zh: 收到了货物。你能帮我个忙，查一下是怎么回事吗？
en: received the goods. Can you do me a favour and find out what's going on?

zh: 好的。让我为您查一下。您能告诉我您的订单号吗？
en: Right. Let me check this for you. Could you give me your order number, please?

zh: 订单号是二八四七五。
en: The order number is two-eight-four-seven-five.

zh: 是的，没错。您需要我确认我的账户信息吗？
en: Yes, that's correct. Would you like me to confirm my account details?

zh: 不，不需要，谢谢。您能确认一下您的姓名吗？
en: No, that's not necessary, thank you. If you could just confirm your name, please?

zh: 好的......嗯，根据我的记录，史密斯女士，您的订单已于上周送达。您还没有收到包裹吗？
en: OK... Well, according to my records, Ms Smith, your order was delivered last week. Have you not received the package?

zh: 没有，恐怕我还没收到。您能再查一下吗？
en: No, I am afraid I haven't. Would you be able to check again?

zh: 当然。哦，这太奇怪了！一定是出了什么差错。对此我非常抱歉。让我向送货员询问一下这个订单的情况。一旦我和他谈过，我可以用这个号码给您回电话吗？
en: Of course. Oh, that's strange! There must have been an error. I'm very sorry about this. Let me enquire about the order with the delivery man. Once I have spoken to him, would it be all right for me to call you back on this number?

zh: 是的，可以，谢谢。
en: Yes, that would be fine, thanks.

zh: 今天还有什么我能帮您的吗？
en: Can I assist you with anything else today?

zh: 没有了，就这些。谢谢您。
en: No, that's all. Thank you.

zh: 您现在收听的是里士满电台。我们请到了来自西伦敦野生动物组织的莎拉·休斯。莎拉，您是在伦敦的这个地区教人们了解自然吗？
en: And you are now on Radio Richmond. We've got Sarah Hughes from West London Wildlife. Sarah, do you teach people about nature in this part of London?

zh: 是的，我们很幸运。实际上，整个城市都相当绿色。大约66%的城市被水或绿地覆盖。很神奇，不是吗？而且还有三百万个私人花园。
en: Yes, we're lucky. Actually, the whole city is quite green. About 66 percent of the city is covered with water or green space. Amazing, isn't it? And there are three million private gardens.

zh: 是的。这对野生动物来说很棒。你可以看到不同种类的鸟——有超过350种。还有其他有趣的动物，比如鹿。我最喜欢的动物是鹿。我们在里士满公园就有它们。
en: Yes. It's great for wildlife. You can see different kinds of birds — there are over 350. And there are other interesting animals too, like deer. My favourite animals are deer. We've got them here in Richmond Park.

zh: 谈到自然，泰晤士河呢？
en: Talking about nature, what about River Thames?

zh: 嗯，现在的水污染比五十年前少了。事实上，河里有大约120种不同的鱼。
en: Well, there's less water pollution now than fifty years ago. In fact, there are about 120 different kinds of fish in the river.

zh: 我认为我们的气候肯定在变化。而且温室效应相当明显。例如，现在的冬天没那么冷了，我们也不常下雪了。
en: I think our climate is definitely changing. And the greenhouse effect is quite obvious. For example, the winters aren't as cold these days and we don't get snow very often.

zh: 最大的环境问题是来自汽车和飞机的空气污染和噪音污染。
en: The biggest environmental problems are air pollution and noise pollution from cars and airplanes.

zh: 伦敦每年有近四十天遭受危险水平的污染。
en: Nearly forty days a year in London suffer from dangerous levels of pollution.

zh: 好的，非常感谢莎拉与我们分享。
en: OK, thanks a lot, Sarah, for sharing with us.

zh: 哇。这个讲座太令人震惊了。
en: Wow. The lecture was so shocking.

zh: 我不知道我们对自然的危害如此之大。
en: I didn't know we were harming nature so much.

zh: 我们试图尽可能多地利用自然，但我们做过头了。
en: We tried to make use of nature as much as we could, but we overdid it.

zh: 自然受到了伤害，感到了痛苦，最终背弃了我们。
en: Nature got hurt, felt pain, and finally turned its back on us.

zh: 是的。自然是我们的家。
en: Yes. Nature is our home.

zh: 人类、动物、植物和所有其他生物都生活在其中。
en: Humans, animals, plants and all other living things live in it.

zh: 我们应该照顾好我们的“家”。
en: We should take care of our "home".

zh: 就像讲座的标题——与自然共生——我们应该保护自然。
en: Just like the title of the lecture — Living with Nature—we should protect nature.

zh: 将来，我们应该......
en: In the future, we should ...

zh: 今天，我们谈论的是从事自然灾害救援的专业团队。
en: Today, we're talking about professional teams who work in natural disaster rescue.

zh: 如你所知，地震、洪水和山体滑坡等自然灾害会造成死亡以及其他难以估量的损失和破坏。
en: As you know, natural disasters like earthquakes, floods and landslides can cause death and other loss and damage that is hard to measure.

zh: 那么，当自然灾害发生时，专业救援团队会做什么？
en: So what do professional rescue teams do when a natural disaster happens?

zh: 今天，来自中国国际救援队的王先生将在这里告诉我们所有关于这方面的事情。
en: Today, Mr Wang from China International Search and Rescue Team is here to tell us all about this.

zh: 王先生，我知道您参与过许多海外以及中国的救援行动，例如2008年的汶川地震。
en: Mr Wang, I know you have been part of many rescue operations overseas, as well as in China, for example the Wenchuan earthquake in 2008.

zh: 那么你们的救援尝试中会发生什么？
en: So what happens in your rescue attempts?

zh: 嗯，在救援尝试中，我们首要和最重要的目标是拯救生命，例如在地震现场。
en: Well, in the rescue attempts our first and most important goal is to save lives, for example at an earthquake site.

zh: 所以我们使用搜救车辆和训练有素的狗来寻找任何还活着的人，并尽力拯救他们。
en: So we use search and rescue vehicles and trained dogs to look for anyone who is still alive and try our best to save them.

zh: 听起来是一个非常具有挑战性的过程。
en: Sounds like a very challenging process.

zh: 一次救援尝试通常需要多长时间？
en: How long does a rescue attempt usually take?

zh: 给你举个例子，我最近的一次尝试是尼泊尔地震。
en: To give you an example, one of my recent attempts was the Nepal earthquake.

zh: 超过六十名成员和六只狗参加了，我们在那里待了十二天。
en: Over sixty members and six dogs took part, and we were there for twelve days.

zh: 我们救出了两个人，并为超过2000人提供了医疗帮助。
en: We rescued two people and provided medical help to over 2,000 people.

zh: 十二天！你们救出了两个人，帮助了2000人！难以想象。
en: Twelve days! You rescued two people and helped 2,000! Hard to imagine.

zh: 那一定非常艰难。
en: It must have been really tough.

zh: 是的。这是一场与时间赛跑的战斗。
en: Yes. It was a battle against time.

zh: 哇，我无法想象你们承受了多大的压力。
en: Wow, I can't imagine how much pressure you are under.

zh: 但尽力减少痛苦和生命损失是我们的职责。
en: But it's our duty to try our best to reduce suffering and the loss of life.

zh: 我们受过训练来做这件事。
en: We're trained to do so.

zh: 王先生，打扰一下，我能在那里打断您吗？
en: Excuse me, Mr Wang, but can I interrupt you there?

zh: 您已经告诉了我们很多关于您如何进行救援尝试的事情，但您能告诉我们更多关于保护的事情吗？
en: You've told us a lot about how you conduct rescue attempts, but could you please tell us more about protection?

zh: 例如，在地震中我们应该做什么？
en: For example, what should we do in an earthquake?

zh: 如果你在户外，就呆在那里，远离任何建筑物或路灯。
en: If you're outdoors, stay there and move away from any buildings or streetlights.

zh: 如果你在室内，远离任何可能破碎或掉落的东西，比如窗户或灯。
en: If you're indoors, stay away from anything that can break or fall, like windows or lamps.

zh: 好的，如果我在户外，就远离任何建筑物或路灯。
en: OK, if I'm outdoors, move away from any buildings or streetlights.

zh: 如果我在室内，远离任何可能破碎或掉落的东西。
en: If I'm indoors, stay away from anything that can break or fall.

zh: 但如果我被困住了呢？
en: But what if I'm trapped?

zh: 我应该尽力大声喊叫，还是......
en: Should I try to shout as loud as I can, or...

zh: 抱歉，但我的建议是敲打墙壁以帮助人们找到你，并且只在附近有人时才喊叫。
en: Sorry, but my advice is to knock on a wall to help people find you and only shout when someone is nearby.

zh: 这是因为喊叫会导致你吸入危险量的灰尘。
en: This is because shouting can cause you to breathe in a dangerous amount of dust.

zh: 我不知道这个——好建议！
en: I didn't know that — great advice!

zh: 我们今天都学到了很多。
en: We've all learnt a lot today.

zh: 嗯，我很想了解更多，但不幸的是，我们的时间用完了。
en: Well, I'd love to know more, but, unfortunately, we've run out of time.

zh: 所以，非常抱歉，但我们不得不就此结束。
en: So, I'm very sorry, but we'll have to finish there.

zh: 再次感谢您，王先生。
en: Thank you again, Mr Wang.

zh: 你心里有钦佩的人吗？
en: Do you have someone in mind that you admire?

zh: 嗯，我认为我的叔叔是我钦佩的人。
en: Well, I think my uncle is someone I admire.

zh: 消防员的工作非常重要。
en: A firefighter's work is very important.

zh: 但你为什么认为消防员是令人钦佩的人？
en: But why do you think firefighters are admirable people?

zh: 我认为他们令人钦佩，因为他们扑灭火灾并拯救人们的生命。
en: I think they are admirable because they put out fires and save people's lives.

zh: 他们还花很多时间帮助处于危险中的人。
en: They also spend lots of time helping people who are in danger.

zh: 他们不仅扑灭火灾，还拯救处于危险中的人。
en: They not only put out fires, but also save people who are in danger.

zh: 我同意，他们做如此重要的工作。
en: They do such important jobs, I agree.

zh: 绝对地，我钦佩他们，因为他们如此勇敢，而且似乎不害怕危险。
en: Absolutely, I admire them because they are so brave and they don't seem to be afraid of danger.

zh: 谈到我们钦佩的人，你认为他们应该具备什么品质？
en: Talking about people we admire, what qualities do you think they should have?

zh: 嗯，有很多。对我来说，我认为他们应该勇敢。
en: Well, there are quite a lot. For me, I think they should be brave.

zh: 他们不害怕危险。
en: They are not afraid of danger.

zh: 他们与邪恶斗争，拯救人们的生命。
en: They fight against evil and save people's lives.

zh: 是的，勇敢很重要，但还有其他重要的事情。
en: Yes, being brave is important, but there are also other important things.

zh: 成为一个令人钦佩的人并不仅仅意味着一直战斗。
en: To be an admirable person doesn't only mean to fight all the time.

zh: 有些人非常努力和富有创造性地工作，让我们的生活更美好，比如科学家。
en: Some people work very hard and creatively to make our lives better, like scientists.

zh: 而且我认为与自身弱点作斗争的人也是令人钦佩的人，比如海伦·凯勒和张海迪。
en: And I think people who fight against their own weaknesses are also admirable people, like Helen Keller and Zhang Haidi.

zh: 你好。我相信每个人都知道马丁·路德·金——一位为美国黑人争取平等权利的伟人。
en: Hello. I'm sure everyone knows about Martin Luther King — a great man who fought to win equal rights for black people in the US.

zh: 今天，我们请来了萨姆·罗伯茨博士来告诉我们更多关于他的事情。
en: Today, we have Dr. Sam Roberts here to tell us more about him.

zh: 那么金小时候是什么样的？
en: So what was King like as a child?

zh: 嗯，他是个聪明的男孩，但他遭受了种族歧视。
en: Well, he was a smart boy, but he suffered racial discrimination.

zh: 那就是人们因为肤色而受到不公平对待。
en: That's when people were treated unfairly due to their skin-colour.

zh: 1943年，金和他的黑人老师一起乘坐公共汽车。
en: In 1943, King took the bus with his black teacher.

zh: 白人司机告诉他们把座位让给两个白人。
en: The white driver told them to give their seats to two white people.

zh: 什么？那他做了什么？
en: What? What did he do then?

zh: 金不想放弃他的座位。
en: King didn't want to give up his seat.

zh: 他相信黑人应该受到和白人一样的待遇。
en: He believed that black people should be treated the same as white people.

zh: 然而，最终，他们不得不这样做。
en: However, in the end, they had to do it.

zh: 他后来对此做了什么吗？
en: Did he do anything about this later?

zh: 1955年，金组织了一次巴士抗议活动，并要求所有黑人停止乘坐巴士。
en: In 1955, King organised a bus protest, and asked all black people to stop taking buses.

zh: 经过一些斗争，他们赢得了在公共汽车上坐在白人旁边的权利。
en: After some struggles, they won the right to sit next to white people on buses.

zh: 这是金的第一次胜利。
en: This was King's first victory.

zh: 金在20世纪60年代组织了进一步的抗议活动，对吗？
en: And King organised further protests in the 1960s, right?

zh: 他组织了许多和平抗议活动，但他因此多次被关进监狱。
en: He organised many peaceful protests, but he was put behind bars many times for doing so.

zh: 1963年，他组织了一次前往华盛顿特区的游行，并发表了一篇题为“我有一个梦想”的演讲。
en: In 1963, he organised a march to Washington, D.C. and made a speech with the words "I have a dream".

zh: 是的，那真是一场著名且鼓舞人心的演讲！
en: Yes, that's a really famous and inspiring speech!

zh: 例如，“我有一个梦想，有一天......我们认为这些真理是不言而喻的：人人生而平等......我有一个梦想，我的四个孩子有一天将生活在一个不是以他们的肤色，而是以他们的品格内涵来评价他们的国度里......”
en: For example, "I have a dream that one day ... We hold these truths to be self-evident: that all men are created equal ... I have a dream that my four little children will one day live in a nation where they will not be judged by the colour of their skin but by the content of their character ..."

zh: 第二年，金获得了诺贝尔和平奖。
en: The next year, King received the Nobel Peace Prize.

zh: 但是，不幸的是，几年后的1968年，他被杀害了。
en: But, unfortunately, he was killed a few years later in 1968.

zh: 这是一个巨大的损失。
en: It was a great loss.

zh: 金将永远因他为帮助美国黑人所做的诸多贡献而被铭记。
en: King will always be remembered for doing a lot to help black people in the US.

zh: 他也激励了世界各地的人们为平等权利和正义而斗争。
en: He has also inspired people around the world to fight for equal rights and justice.

zh: 所以，对于我们的学校项目，也许我们可以谈谈圣雄甘地？
en: So, for our school project, maybe we can talk about Mahatma Gandhi?

zh: 我真的很钦佩和尊敬他，毫无疑问他是20世纪最重要的人物之一。
en: I really admire and respect him, and there's no doubt that he was one of the most important figures of the 20th century.

zh: 让我记些笔记。
en: Let me write down some notes.

zh: 那么，我们先谈谈他为什么如此重要。
en: So, let's first talk about why he was so important.

zh: 嗯，甘地领导印度从英国统治下获得独立......嗯，我不确定具体日期。
en: Well, Gandhi led India to independence from British rule ... mmm, I'm not sure about the exact date.

zh: 哦，我可以用手机查一下......印度在1947年成为一个独立国家。
en: Oh, I can check this on my phone... India became an independent country in 1947.

zh: 甘地之所以重要，还因为他相信无人受伤的和平抗议的力量。
en: Gandhi was important also because he believed in the power of peaceful protests where no one was hurt.

zh: 他激励了很多人，包括马丁·路德·金。
en: He inspired many people, including Martin Luther King.

zh: 你确定吗？
en: Are you sure about that?

zh: 我前几天在一个广播节目中学到的。
en: I learnt this on a radio programme the other day.

zh: 我不知道这个。
en: I didn't know about that.

zh: 我想我们还应该提到甘地的诺贝尔和平奖。
en: And I guess we should also mention Gandhi's Nobel Peace Prize.

zh: 实际上，我认为甘地从未获得过诺贝尔和平奖。
en: Actually, I don't think Gandhi ever won a Nobel Peace Prize.

zh: 嗯......我不知道这是不是真的......
en: Mmm...I don't know if that's true ...

zh: 我很确定他没有。
en: I'm pretty sure he didn't.

zh: 看，这里，读一下这个网页。
en: Look, here, read this web page.

zh: 但也许我们应该再查几个网站。
en: But perhaps we should check a few other websites.

zh: 有时网上的信息可能是错的。
en: Sometimes the information on the Internet can be wrong.

zh: 让我再找一些来源......
en: Let me find a few more sources ...
